title: Dufourea purpurascens
taxonomy: Dufourea purpurascens is a species of bee fly in the family Halictidae. It is a type of hymenopteran, a group of insects that also includes bees, ants, and wasps. As a member of the family Halictidae, Dufourea purpurascens is closely related to other small, metallic-colored bees and bee flies. Within the genus Dufourea, Dufourea purpurascens is further classified as a species of solitary bee fly, characterized by its solitary lifestyle and lack of social organization. Dufourea purpurascens is also known to be a member of the order Diptera, which includes flies, gnats, and mosquitoes.

intro: Dufourea purpurascens, also known as Red Dufourea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Halictidae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from inflammation, pain, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The bioactive constituents of Dufourea purpurascens include essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dufourea purpurascens include teas, infusions, and salves, often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions.
